game.name: Lunar Lockout
game.board: The game has a 7x7 game board, where only 5x5 include legal moves. The boarder edge cells of the 5x5 is considered the space. Robots that slide through space are no longer able to get back to the board.
game.pieces: There is one msin red robot and 4 to 5 helper robots of different colors.
game.tomove: Slide robots to different directions in the board. They move up to a blocker robot.
game.towin: To win, have the red robot reach the center cell.
game.rules: All robots, regardless of type, are able to slide throughout the entire 5x5 board. For a robot to slide and not fall off space, they need to have another robot in front of them as a blocker. Thus, for the red robot to reach the base, it needs to have a helper robot as a blocker one cell next to the center. If a robot falls of space, outside of the 5x5 board, then they are in space and are no longer able to get back to the board. The game can still be winnable if a helper robot is thrown out of space. However, if the red robot leaves the board, the game is now over.

variant.name: Beginner
variant.description: Most simple variant with 4 helper robots.

variant.name: Easy
variant.description: Slightly more difficult variant with 4 helper robots.

variant.name: Medium
variant.description: Medium difficulty variant with 4 helper robots.

variant.name: Hard
variant.description: Most difficult variant with 5 helper robots.
